# Bubbaloop

> Open-source Hardware AI agent. Single Rust binary for cameras, sensors, robots, and IoT fleets — orchestrated by AI agents with memory and real-time telemetry.

Bubbaloop is a physical AI orchestration platform built on Zenoh pub/sub. It runs as a single ~13MB binary on edge devices (Jetson, Raspberry Pi, any Linux) and provides:

- **Multi-agent runtime** with Soul identity, 4-tier memory (world state, short-term, episodic, semantic), and adaptive heartbeat
- **42 MCP tools** with 3-tier RBAC for node lifecycle, data queries, agent memory, beliefs, missions, and constraints
- **Zenoh data plane** for microsecond-latency pub/sub between sensor nodes, actuators, and AI agents
- **Node SDK** (Rust + Python) for building self-describing nodes in ~50 lines of code
- **Telemetry watchdog** with 5 severity levels and automatic circuit breakers
- **Web dashboard** for real-time visualization of camera feeds, sensor data, and system metrics

## Documentation

- [Quickstart](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/getting-started/quickstart/): Install and run in 30 seconds
- [Installation](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/getting-started/installation/): Platform-specific install instructions
- [Configuration](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/getting-started/configuration/): YAML-based node and system configuration
- [Architecture](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/concepts/architecture/): Runtime design, daemon subsystems, security layers
- [Messaging](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/concepts/messaging/): Zenoh pub/sub patterns, topic conventions, wire format
- [Memory](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/concepts/memory/): 4-tier agent memory and mission engine
- [Topics](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/concepts/topics/): Key expression hierarchy and naming conventions
- [Agent Guide](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/agent-guide/): Multi-agent setup, Soul, identity, capabilities
- [Create Your First Node](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/guides/create-your-first-node/): Step-by-step Rust node tutorial
- [Node Marketplace](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/guides/node-marketplace/): Browse and install community nodes
- [Telemetry Watchdog](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/guides/telemetry-watchdog/): Resource monitoring and circuit breakers
- [CLI Reference](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/reference/cli/): Complete command reference
- [API Reference](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/api/): Protobuf schemas and message formats
- [Dashboard](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/dashboard/): Web UI for real-time visualization
- [Troubleshooting](https://kornia.org/bubbaloop/troubleshooting/): Common issues and solutions

## Key Concepts

### Node Contract
Every node serves five standard Zenoh queryables: schema (FileDescriptorSet), manifest (capabilities), health (status), config (YAML), and command (imperative actions). Agents discover nodes via `bubbaloop/**/manifest` wildcard queries.

### Topic Hierarchy
```
bubbaloop/{key_space}/{machine_id}/{node_name}/{resource}
```
- `global` = network-visible (dashboard, CLI, remote machines)
- `local` = SHM-only (same-machine, never crosses WebSocket bridge)

### MCP Integration
Run `bubbaloop mcp --stdio` for Claude Code integration, or use the daemon's HTTP endpoint on :8088 for remote agents. 42 tools across 11 categories: Discovery, Lifecycle, Data, System, Memory, Beliefs, World State, Context Providers, Missions, Constraints, Alerts.

### Node SDK
```rust
use bubbaloop_node::{Node, NodeContext, run_node};

struct MySensor;

#[async_trait]
impl Node for MySensor {
    async fn init(ctx: &NodeContext) -> anyhow::Result<Self> { Ok(Self) }
    async fn run(self, ctx: NodeContext) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
        let publisher = ctx.publisher_json("data").await?;
        // publish sensor data...
        Ok(())
    }
}

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { run_node::<MySensor>().await.unwrap(); }
```
